title: Voice Notes
description: User-recorded audio notes paired with their auto-generated transcript.

ai.purpose: Stores dictaphone recordings: the audio blob (as a `files` row) plus the transcribed text so the note is searchable and readable without playback.
ai.howItWorks: The `VoiceDictaphonePrefab` records audio in the browser, calls `/voice-api/api/stt` to transcribe, then on save uploads the audio to core's `/api/files/uploadDirect` and creates a `voice_notes` row linking `audioFileId` to the resulting file and storing the (optionally edited) `transcript`.
ai.manipulation: Create rows through the dictaphone prefab. Editing `transcript` later is fine â€” it's just the cached text. Don't edit `audioFileId` unless you mean to swap the underlying recording.

outcomes.how: Open the Dictaphone, press Record, stop, optionally edit the transcript, click Save. A `voice_notes` row is created with `audioFileId` pointing at the new `files` record.

warnings: Deleting a `voice_notes` row does NOT delete the underlying `files` record. Delete the file separately if needed.

title.title: Title
title.description: Short label for this note. Defaults to capture timestamp.

audioFileId.title: Audio
audioFileId.description: The recorded audio stored in core's `files` collection.

transcript.title: Transcript
transcript.description: What was said in the recording. Auto-generated via STT, user-editable before save.

language.title: Language
language.description: ISO 639-1 language code detected during transcription (e.g. `en`).

durationMs.title: Duration (ms)
durationMs.description: Length of the recording in milliseconds.

mimeType.title: MIME type
mimeType.description: MIME type of the audio blob (mirrors `files.mimeType` for fast list rendering).

title: Voices
description: Catalog of TTS voice profiles available through the voice app's OmniVoice backend.

ai.purpose: Stores the voice profiles users can choose from for text-to-speech playback. Each row pairs a reference audio file + transcript that voice-api uses to provision an OmniVoice voice profile.
ai.howItWorks: Source of truth lives here. `voice-api` runs a reconciler that, for each row with `audioFileId` + `refText`, downloads the audio from core's `files` collection and POSTs it to OmniVoice `/profiles`, then writes the resulting `profile_id` back to `profileId` on this row. `VoiceSpeakerPrefab` looks up the user's preferred voice (stored on `user_ui_configs.voice.selectedVoiceId`) and forwards `profileId` to OmniVoice `/generate`. Because the reference audio is persisted in core (not in the OmniVoice container's volume), profiles survive stack rebuilds â€” the reconciler re-provisions them on startup.
ai.manipulation: To add a voice, upload a 3+ second reference WAV via the standard core file uploader (which creates a `files` record), then create a `voice_voices` row with `displayName`, `audioFileId` set to the file's `_id`, and `refText` set to the audio's transcript. Leave `profileId` empty â€” the reconciler fills it in. Use `enabled=false` to hide a voice from pickers without deleting it. `order` controls picker sort.

outcomes.outcome: Add a voice from a reference audio sample
outcomes.how: Upload the WAV via the standard files uploader to get a `files._id`. Create a `voice_voices` row with `audioFileId` set to that id, `refText` set to what's spoken in the audio, plus `displayName`, `language`, `gender`. The reconciler clones the profile in OmniVoice and back-fills `profileId` within ~1s.
Set `enabled=false`. The catalog row stays for traceability and the reconciler stops touching it.

warnings: Don't manually edit `profileId` â€” it's backend-managed by the reconciler. Editing it points the row at a stale OmniVoice profile that may not exist after a rebuild.
`audioFileId` MUST point at a real `files` record the voice-api functional user can read (admin/user group, not private). Private files won't be visible to the reconciler.
Do not delete a voice that users have selected as their preference â€” flip `enabled=false` instead, so the prefab can fall back gracefully.

key.title: Key
key.description: Stable unique slug for this voice (typically matches `profileId`).

displayName.title: Display name
displayName.description: User-facing label shown in voice pickers.

engine.title: Engine
engine.description: TTS engine providing this voice. Reserved for future engines.

profileId.title: Profile ID
profileId.description: Engine-specific profile identifier. Backend-managed: the voice-api reconciler fills this in after cloning a profile in OmniVoice from `audioFileId` + `refText`. Leave empty when creating a new voice; do not hand-edit.

provisionedFromAudioFileId.title: Provisioned from audio
provisionedFromAudioFileId.description: Backend-managed drift marker: the `audioFileId` that was used to clone the current `profileId`. When it differs from the current `audioFileId`, the reconciler deletes the stale OmniVoice profile and re-provisions.

provisionedFromRefText.title: Provisioned from refText
provisionedFromRefText.description: Backend-managed drift marker: the `refText` that was used to clone the current `profileId`. Mismatch triggers re-provisioning.

provisionedFromSeed.title: Provisioned seed
provisionedFromSeed.description: Backend-managed drift marker: deterministic seed pinned on the OmniVoice profile so repeat TTS calls produce a consistent voice. Missing value on an existing profile triggers re-provisioning.

audioFileId.title: Reference audio
audioFileId.description: Reference WAV used to clone this voice in OmniVoice. 3+ seconds of clean human speech recommended. Upload via the standard files uploader, then pick the resulting file here.

refText.title: Reference text
refText.description: Verbatim transcript of `audioFileId`. Used by OmniVoice to clone the voice â€” needs to match the audio exactly for best quality.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Hide from pickers without deleting when false.
